title: Task Sync Disappearing File Resilience Acceptance Contract

entries.summary: The trace carries a fully authored planning chain, accepted decision, acceptance contract, planning-baseline evidence, and bounded task set for the disappearing-file resilience slice.
entries.notes: Bootstrap baseline contract prepared at 2026-03-12T12:53:02Z for trace.task_sync_disappearing_file_resilience.

entries.summary: Task-document iteration tolerates a discovered task file disappearing before load and the shared task sync surfaces continue on the surviving task set.
entries.notes: Implementation acceptance is satisfied only when the disappearing-file boundary is handled at the shared iterator layer without changing the task lifecycle contract.

entries.summary: Regression coverage proves the iterator skips only the vanished task path and keeps surviving task-document validation intact.
entries.notes: Regression acceptance covers the reproduced disappearing-file scenario and keeps stable task-document validation fail-closed.

entries.summary: Final validation and a follow-up expansive review pass find no further issues in the affected task-sync orchestration area.
entries.notes: Closeout acceptance is satisfied only when the full validation baseline and a second orchestration review pass both finish without new findings.
